intent: Write a 3-source deep brief on 'choosing a mechanical keyboard for long typing sessions' under $150. (1) One Stop Market shopping site — browse the electronics and office-products categories and collect at least 4 keyboard or keyboard-adjacent products (keyboard, wrist rest, keycap set, mouse for pair). Record name, price, rating, review count. (2) Reddit — browse /f/technology AND /f/gaming for posts on switch types, build quality, or regrets. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, comment count. (3) Wikipedia — open 'Keyboard technology' or 'Mechanical keyboard' (whichever exists) and extract 3-4 facts about switch mechanisms and layouts. The report must: (a) present the storefront picks with stats, (b) match switch/feature choices to Wikipedia-grounded differences, (c) integrate community warnings about specific sound or travel-distance tradeoffs, (d) conclude with a single $150 recommendation. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Hard 3-site. Keyboard switch-tech grounding + community aesthetics.
